preamble: The hardening of a glottal stop to a bilabial stop is a rare form of fortition, where a 'placeholder' or weak laryngeal sound develops a full oral place of articulation.

examples.note: Glottal or weak final sounds in English loanwords hardened to full labial stops (p/b).
Historical glottal stops or laryngealized vowels shifted to voiced or voiceless stops.

sources: Lass, R. (1984). Phonology: An Introduction to Basic Concepts. Cambridge University Press.
